label: Glass Frosted
description: Modern SaaS analytics dashboard with frosted surfaces and layered depth.
signatureMotif: frosted panels, layered cards, and subtle glowing metrics
promptHints: Keep information density high, but use translucent depth to separate zones cleanly.
This should feel operational, polished, and product-grade rather than brochure-like.

layouts: Use a fixed frosted top bar and translucent left rail, with the main canvas arranged as a 12-column analytics grid.
Build the overview page with a wide KPI row, a dominant central trend chart, and stacked right-side activity cards.
Compose detail pages as split workspaces with a primary data panel left and floating utility cards layered on the right.
Use modal and drawer flows as elevated glass sheets above a dim dashboard, keeping surrounding metrics faintly visible through blur.
Arrange settings and management pages as dense card matrices with grouped sections, short forms, and inline status panels instead of long single columns.
motifs: Use translucent charcoal panels with strong backdrop blur, 1rem radius, hairline borders, and soft internal highlights on edges.
Pair deep blue-violet backgrounds with cyan or violet metric glows, making key numbers feel softly illuminated without looking neon-gaming.
Layer cards at multiple elevations with subtle shadows and slight tint shifts, so every zone feels stacked like product UI glass.
Use crisp sans typography, compact spacing, and mini charts embedded inside cards to keep the interface operational and information-rich.
antiPatterns: Avoid warm paper textures, grainy organic surfaces, or hand-drawn accents that break the polished product-grade dashboard feel.
Never use flat opaque white cards or bright light-mode sections that destroy the layered frosted depth across the workspace.
Avoid oversized marketing hero sections with sparse copy and giant illustrations; this variant should read as an active application first.
Do not introduce loud rainbow gradients or glossy 3D blobs that overpower charts, metrics, and functional navigation.
